section: Section 139 — Filing of Income Tax Return
text: Every person whose total income during the previous year exceeded the basic exemption limit is required to file an income tax return. For individuals below 60 years, the basic exemption limit is Rs 2.5 lakh (old regime) or Rs 3 lakh (new regime under Section 115BAC). The due date for filing ITR for individuals is 31st July of the assessment year. Late filing attracts a penalty of Rs 5,000 under Section 234F (Rs 1,000 if income below Rs 5 lakh). You can file ITR online at incometax.gov.in. TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) deducted from salary or other income appears in Form 26AS and AIS.

section: Section 80C — Deductions from Gross Total Income
text: Section 80C allows deductions up to Rs 1,50,000 per year for investments in: Life Insurance Premium, PPF (Public Provident Fund), ELSS (Equity Linked Savings Scheme), NSC (National Savings Certificate), 5-year fixed deposits with banks, home loan principal repayment, tuition fees for children, Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana. Section 80D allows deduction for health insurance premiums up to Rs 25,000 (Rs 50,000 for senior citizens). Section 80G allows deduction for donations to notified charitable institutions.

After filing your ITR, the Income Tax Department may send a notice under Section 143(2) for scrutiny assessment within six months from the end of the financial year. You must respond to such notices within the time given. Section 148 notice means the department believes income has escaped assessment — you must file ITR in response. Section 245 notice means refund is being adjusted against outstanding tax demand. Always respond to IT notices on time to avoid penalty. You can raise a grievance on the e-filing portal.

GST (Goods and Services Tax) is a unified indirect tax that replaced VAT, Service Tax, Excise Duty and other taxes from 1st July 2017. GST has four slabs: 5%, 12%, 18%, and 28%. Essential food items are exempt (0%). GST is collected by both Centre (CGST) and State (SGST) for intra-state supplies. IGST applies on inter-state supplies. Every business with annual turnover above Rs 40 lakh (Rs 20 lakh for services) must register for GST. GST registration is done on the GST portal (gst.gov.in). GSTIN is a 15-digit number assigned upon registration.

section: Section 16 — Eligibility for Input Tax Credit
text: Every registered person shall be entitled to take credit of input tax charged on any supply of goods or services which are used or intended to be used in the course or furtherance of his business. Conditions for ITC: (1) You must have a valid tax invoice (2) You must have received the goods or services (3) Tax must have been paid by the supplier to the government (4) You must have filed your GST return. ITC is NOT allowed on personal expenses, food, beverages, beauty treatment, health services, and motor vehicles used for non-business purposes.
